summary: PR #2269 replaced four fighter placeholder functions with matched C implementations in Kirby and Yoshi special-move files. The PR body claims all four are 100% matched, and the bot report records four new 100% matches totaling +484 matched bytes.

key_files.role: Matched ftKb_SpecialAirHi2_Anim and ftKb_SpecialNLg_800F951C, replacing two placeholder comments with C implementations.
evidence_refs: changed_files: ftKb_SpecialN.c added 32 deleted 2 in 2 hunks
text_corpus: bot report lists both Kirby functions as 100.00% after

key_files.role: Matched ftYs_SpecialHi_Enter and ftYs_SpecialAirHi_Enter, replacing two placeholder comments with C implementations.
evidence_refs: changed_files: ftYs_SpecialHi.c added 34 deleted 2 in 1 hunk
text_corpus: bot report lists both Yoshi functions as 100.00% after

what_changed.text: Implemented ftKb_SpecialAirHi2_Anim: gets Fighter via GET_FIGHTER, waits for no remaining animation frames, changes motion state to ftKb_MS_SpecialAirHi3 with flags 0xA, then assigns pre_hitlag_cb to efLib_PauseAll and post_hitlag_cb to efLib_ResumeAll.

Implemented ftKb_SpecialNLg_800F951C: calls ftCommon_8007D5D4, preserves current animation frame, selects motion_id 0x190 or 0x201 based on fp2->fv.kb.hat.kind, changes motion state with flags 0x5000, and assigns accessory4_cb to fn_800F9260.

Implemented ftYs_SpecialHi_Enter and ftYs_SpecialAirHi_Enter with nearly identical setup: zero Yoshi special-hi move fields, clear fv.ys.x2238, throw_flags, and cmd_vars[0], change to grounded or aerial SpecialHi motion state, set accessory4_cb to fn_8012E110, and call ftAnim_8006EBA4.

The automated report records four new matches: ftKb_SpecialNLg_800F951C (+124 bytes), ftKb_SpecialAirHi2_Anim (+120 bytes), ftYs_SpecialHi_Enter (+120 bytes), and ftYs_SpecialAirHi_Enter (+120 bytes), moving each from 0.00% to 100.00%.

smart_moves.text: Used typed Fighter fields and character-specific union arms rather than raw offsets for state setup and checks, including fp->mv.ys.specialhi, fp->fv.ys.x2238, fp->fv.kb.hat.kind, throw_flags, cmd_vars, cur_anim_frame, and callback fields.

Kept the grounded and aerial Yoshi SpecialHi enter functions as parallel source forms with only the motion-state enum differing, matching repeated local behavior without over-abstracting into a helper.
For Kirby's inhale transition, used a simple local motion_id variable initialized to the default and conditionally overwritten when the Kirby hat kind is nonzero.

decomp_lessons.lesson: Small fighter action-state functions can often match cleanly by expressing state transitions through Fighter_ChangeMotionState plus typed callback and per-character FighterVar fields.
When grounded and aerial variants are structurally identical, a direct parallel implementation with only the motion-state constant changed may be the matching form.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: bot report lists both Yoshi variants as 100.00%

decomp_lessons.lesson: Function-local temporary names can stay conservative when semantics are only partly known; this PR keeps address-style helper names such as fn_800F9260 and fn_8012E110 while using role names for clear locals like fp, motion_id, and anim_frame.

naming_conventions.name: gobj
naming_conventions.meaning: Fighter_GObj* function parameter for fighter action callbacks.

naming_conventions.name: fp
naming_conventions.meaning: Fighter* local for the current fighter object, commonly recovered via GET_FIGHTER(gobj) or gobj->user_data in this slice.

naming_conventions.name: fp2
naming_conventions.meaning: Second Fighter* local in ftKb_SpecialNLg_800F951C, assigned from gobj->user_data and used for the Kirby hat-kind check.

naming_conventions.name: motion_id
naming_conventions.meaning: Local s32 used to hold the selected motion-state id before Fighter_ChangeMotionState.

naming_conventions.name: anim_frame
naming_conventions.meaning: Local f32 preserving fp->cur_anim_frame across a motion-state transition.
Anonymous u8 stack-padding local used in the two matched Yoshi enter functions; evidence supports its use in this PR but not as a general style rule.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Use explicit stack padding u8 _[24] in ftYs_SpecialHi_Enter and ftYs_SpecialAirHi_Enter.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: both Yoshi enter functions contain u8 _[24]
text_corpus: bot report lists both Yoshi functions as 100.00%
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caveat: The PR evidence shows the tactic in matched functions, but does not include objdiff details explaining why the padding is required; do not generalize without local verifier evidence.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Re-fetch the Fighter pointer with GET_FIGHTER(gobj) after Fighter_ChangeMotionState before assigning accessory4_cb in both Yoshi enter functions.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: bot report lists both Yoshi functions as 100.00%
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caveat: Evidence supports this exact matched shape in the two functions; broader intent is not documented.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: For Kirby SpecialNLg, preserve fp->cur_anim_frame in a local before the motion-state call and select the motion id with an if over fp2->fv.kb.hat.kind.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: bot report lists ftKb_SpecialNLg_800F951C as 100.00%
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caveat: No review discussion explains whether the local ordering was required for codegen.

follow_up_queries: fighter special hi enter re-fetch GET_FIGHTER after Fighter_ChangeMotionState

accepted_candidate_records.title: Typed fighter fields and callbacks can match small action-state functions
accepted_candidate_records.text: In PR #2269, four small fighter special-move functions matched by using typed Fighter fields, per-character union arms, Fighter_ChangeMotionState, and callback assignments rather than raw offsets.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Grounded and aerial fighter enter functions may need parallel source shapes
accepted_candidate_records.text: The matched Yoshi SpecialHi grounded and aerial enter functions are nearly identical, differing mainly in the motion-state enum passed to Fighter_ChangeMotionState.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: bot report both Yoshi functions 100.00%

rejection_notes: Do not promote u8 _[24] stack padding as a general standard; the slice only shows it in two matched Yoshi functions and provides no detailed objdiff rationale.
Do not infer reviewer intent or review approval rationale; no review comments were loaded.
Do not infer that address-style callbacks fn_800F9260 and fn_8012E110 have known semantic names; the PR kept conservative address-style names.
